full_response:
We are given the projection operator \(P_{ab} = g_{ab} + U_a U_b\), where \(U^a\) is a velocity vector satisfying \(U^a U_a = -1\) (timelike unit vector in the \((-,+,+,+)\) signature). Define \(P^a_{\, b} = g^{ac} P_{cb} = \delta^a_b + U^a U_b\). For any vector \(V^a\), the orthogonal projection is \(V^a_\perp = P^a_{\, b} V^b\).

### Proof of Orthogonality
Compute the contraction \(V^a_\perp U_a\):
\[
V^a_\perp U_a = (V^a + U^a (U_b V^b)) U_a = V^a U_a + (U^a U_a)(U_b V^b).
\]
Since \(U^a U_a = -1\) and \(U_b V^b\) is a scalar \(S\), we have:
\[
V^a_\perp U_a = S + (-1)S = 0.
\]
Thus, \(V^a_\perp\) is orthogonal to \(U^a\).

### Proof of Idempotence
First compute \(V^b_\perp = P^b_{\, c} V^c = V^b + U^b (U_c V^c)\). Then apply the projection again:
\[
P^a_{\, b} V^b_\perp = (\delta^a_b + U^a U_b)[V^b + U^b S] = \delta^a_b(V^b + U^b S) + U^a U_b(V^b + U^b S).
\]
Simplify each term:
\[
\delta^a_b(V^b + U^b S) = V^a + U^a S,
\]
\[
U^a U_b(V^b + U^b S) = U^a (U_b V^b) + U^a (U_b U^b) S = U^a S + U^a (-1)S = U^a S - U^a S = 0.
\]
Thus,
\[
P^a_{\, b} V^b_\perp = V^a + U^a S = V^a_\perp.
\]
Hence, the projection operator leaves \(V^a_\perp\) unchanged.

Both properties are verified.

\boxed{\text{The vector } V^a_\perp = P^a_{\, b} V^b \text{ satisfies } V^a_\perp U_a = 0 \text{ and } P^a_{\, b} V^b_\perp = V^a_\perp.}

Confidence: 100%
extracted_answer: \text{The vector } V^a_\perp = P^a_{\, b} V^b \text{ satisfies } V^a_\perp U_a = 0 \text{ and } P^a_{\, b} V^b_\perp = V^a_\perp.
